Municipal water is treated at a water treatment plant before it is sent through pipes to homes and businesses. The water is treated with chemicals to kill any harmful bacteria or viruses that may be present. The water is also filtered to remove any dirt, debris, or other particles that may be present. The pipes that the water travels through are made of materials that do not corrode or allow bacteria to grow, and the water is constantly moving through the pipes, which helps to keep the inside of the pipes clean. Additionally, the water is tested regularly to ensure that it meets safety standards for drinking water. So even though the inside of the pipes may not be visibly clean, the water that we drink is safe because it has been treated and tested to ensure that it is free of harmful contaminants.

The Appalachian Trail is a long-distance hiking trail that runs from Springer Mountain in Georgia to Mount Katahdin in Maine. Along the way, it passes through a number of mountain ranges, including the Great Smoky Mountains, the Blue Ridge Mountains, the White Mountains, and the Green Mountains. Some of the specific mountains that the trail passes over or near include Blood Mountain, Clingmans Dome, Mount Washington, and Saddleback Mountain. These are just a few examples, as there are many other mountains and peaks along the trail.

In the United States, the Senate is one of the two chambers of Congress, the other being the House of Representatives. The Constitution specifies that each state will have two senators, and it also specifies that the terms of senators will be six years. To ensure that the Senate remains representative of the people, one-third of the Senate is elected every two years in what are called "Senate elections." This means that every two years, there are elections for about one-third of the seats in the Senate.

This system was established by the Founders of the United States to ensure that the Senate would be a more stable and deliberative body than the House of Representatives, which is elected every two years and therefore tends to be more responsive to the changing views of the public. By having longer terms and only electing a portion of its members at a time, the Senate is able to take a longer-term view of policy issues and is less likely to be swayed by short-term political considerations.

In the United Kingdom, the parliament is made up of the House of Commons and the House of Lords. Members of the House of Commons are elected every five years, while members of the House of Lords are not elected at all. Instead, they are appointed by the government or inherited their titles.

A backslash () and a forward slash (/) are both used in different contexts to represent different things. In general, a forward slash is used to separate things, such as in a URL where it is used to separate different parts of the address. For example, in the URL "https://www.example.com/pages/about", the forward slash is used to separate the protocol (https), the domain name (www.example.com), and the path to a specific page (/pages/about). On the other hand, a backslash is often used in computer programming to represent the start of a special character or code. For example, in some programming languages, a backslash followed by the letter "n" represents a new line, and a backslash followed by the letter "t" represents a tab. In addition, a backslash is also used in some contexts to represent a folder or directory on a computer, such as in a file path like "C:\Users\Username\Documents\file.txt". In this case, the backslashes are used to separate the different folders in the file path. So, to summarize, a forward slash is used to separate things, while a backslash is often used to represent special characters or to separate folders in a file path.

A business is generally free to choose what forms of payment it will accept, and some businesses may choose not to accept cash. This can be for a variety of reasons, such as to reduce the risk of theft or to make it easier to track sales for tax or accounting purposes. Some businesses may also choose to only accept electronic forms of payment, such as debit or credit cards, in order to reduce the need for handling physical currency.However, it is important to note that in some cases, a business may be required by law to accept cash as a form of payment. For example, in the United States, the federal government has passed legislation that prohibits businesses from discriminating against customers based on the type of payment they use. This means that businesses must accept cash as a form of payment unless there is a legitimate reason for not doing so.In general, it is a good idea for businesses to clearly communicate their payment policies to customers, whether through signage, on their website, or through other means. This can help to avoid misunderstandings and ensure that customers are aware of the types of payment that are accepted.

Side work, also known as freelance or gig work, can be a good way to earn additional income and gain new skills. However, it's important to manage your finances carefully when doing side work, as you may not have the same level of financial stability or benefits as you would with a traditional full-time job. Here are a few tips for managing your finances when doing side work:Create a budget: Determine how much money you need to cover your basic expenses, such as rent, utilities, and groceries, and set aside a portion of your earnings for these expenses.Save for taxes: Since you are self-employed, you are responsible for paying your own taxes. Set aside a portion of your earnings for taxes each month to ensure you have enough money when it's time to file your tax return.Build an emergency fund: It's important to have savings set aside for unexpected expenses or emergencies. Consider setting a goal to save a certain amount of money each month in an emergency fund.Keep good records: Keep track of your income and expenses for tax purposes. This will help you accurately report your earnings and expenses when you file your tax return.Consider insurance: If you are doing side work, you may not have the same level of insurance coverage as you would with a traditional job. Consider purchasing health insurance, disability insurance, and other types of insurance to protect yourself and your finances.

The effective interest rate is the interest rate that is actually paid on a loan or earned on an investment, taking into account the compounding of interest over time. It is a way to compare the true cost of borrowing or the true return on an investment, taking into account the frequency of compounding.If you have a bifurcated interest rate, it means that the interest rate is divided into two parts, each with a different rate. For example, a bifurcated interest rate might be made up of a lower rate for the first year of the loan, followed by a higher rate for the remaining term of the loan. In this case, the effective interest rate would be the weighted average of the two rates, taking into account the length of time each rate applies.To calculate the effective interest rate, you would need to know the terms of the loan, including the two interest rates and the length of time each rate applies, as well as the frequency of compounding. You can then use a financial calculator or a spreadsheet program to calculate the effective interest rate.It's important to note that the effective interest rate is not the same as the nominal interest rate, which is the stated interest rate without taking into account the effect of compounding. The effective interest rate is usually higher than the nominal interest rate, because it takes into account the compounding of interest over time.

Yes, that's correct! Water at the bottom of the ocean is more dense than water at the top because of the pressure. Imagine you have a big jar full of water. If you put a rock at the bottom of the jar, the water will push down on the rock with a certain amount of force. This force is called pressure. Now imagine that you put the same rock at the top of the jar. The water will still push down on the rock, but there will be less water above it, so the force, or pressure, will be less. The same thing happens in the ocean. The water at the bottom of the ocean is under a lot of pressure because there is a lot of water above it. This makes the water more dense, which means there are more molecules packed into a certain space. On the other hand, the water at the top of the ocean is under less pressure, so it is less dense. Dark matter is a mysterious substance that scientists believe exists because of the way that galaxies behave. It doesn't give off any light, which is why it's called "dark," and it doesn't interact with regular matter in the same way that things like atoms do. Scientists believe that dark matter makes up a large portion of the universe, but we can't see it or touch it directly. We can only detect its presence by how it affects the way other objects move. One way scientists think dark matter might be able to affect things is by providing a kind of "gravitational glue" that holds galaxies together. Without dark matter, galaxies might fly apart because of the pull of gravity. We can't directly affect dark matter because it doesn't interact with regular matter in the same way. It's kind of like trying to catch a ball that's made out of rubber - no matter how hard you try, it just bounces off your hand. That's why it's so difficult for us to study dark matter - we can't directly observe it or experiment with it. But even though we don't fully understand dark matter yet, scientists are working hard to learn more about it and figure out its properties. Some scientists think that dark matter might be made up of particles we don't yet know about, and they're using superpowerful telescopes and other tools to try and find evidence of these particles.

There are some species that do n't die of old age ( search for " immortal jellyfish " ) . Reptiles do n't experience nearly the physical decline that mammals do during old age . I heard somewhere that mammals tend to live for about 800 million heartbeats ( smaller species have more rapid heart rates ) . I have always assumed that a relatively short life span might be advantageous to some species as it allowed them to evolve more quickly to adapt to changing environmental conditions . Humans are not the end goal of evolution . We are not " ideally suited " to our environment . Like most animals , our bodies are just good enough to pass along our genes to the next generation and raise our offspring to reproductive age ( if we are lucky ) , before they really start to give out . I strongly believe that humans will figure out how to slow down the aging process and eventually reverse it . I do n't think this will happen in the next 50 years , but it will almost certainly happen in the next 100 years . Personally , I am glad I will not be around to see humans conquer old age . Reducing the negative effects of aging and slightly lengthening life span is fine , but indefinite natural lifespan would create serious problems . Here are a few : 1 . At first , only rich people will be able to afford to live indefinitely . This will create resentment , and could prompt many ultra - rich people to completely isolate themselves ( and their money ) from the rest of society . 2 . " Immortal " humans ( at least the non - robotic ones ) will still be susceptible to accidental death , violent crime , possibly certain virulent diseases , etc . This may lead to an aversion to dangerous activities and further separation between " immortals " and the general population . 3 . If the " immortality treatment " becomes affordable to the masses , the real problems begin . Future governments would either have to ban pregnancy or ban immortality in order to avoid overpopulation . 4 . The human mind may not be able to handle hundreds of years of life without becoming bored or burned out ( or just plain crazy ) . There may be drugs or electronic implants ( co - processors ) to alleviate these problems , but the brain may be the one organ that can not be solved .

A file-based system is a method of organizing and storing data in a computer system. In a file-based system, data is stored in individual files, and these files are stored in directories (also called folders). Each file is given a unique name and is stored in a specific location within the directory structure.

In a file-based system, users can create, modify, and delete files and directories as needed. They can also move files from one directory to another, copy files, and perform other operations on the files.

File-based systems are commonly used in operating systems, such as Windows, macOS, and Linux, to manage the storage and organization of data on a computer. They provide a flexible and easy-to-use method for storing and accessing data, and they are a key component of many computer systems.

In storytelling, an arc refers to the progression or development of a character or the plot of a story. It's a way of describing the internal and external changes that a character goes through over the course of a narrative.

An arc can refer to the overall shape of a story, with a beginning, middle, and end, as well as the smaller arcs within the story, such as the arcs of individual characters or subplots.

In a character arc, the character begins in one place and ends in another, having undergone some form of change or growth. This can be a change in the character's beliefs, attitudes, behavior, or circumstances. For example, a character who starts out selfish and self-centered may undergo a transformation and become more empathetic and compassionate by the end of the story.

In a plot arc, the story follows a sequence of events that build upon each other and ultimately lead to a resolution or conclusion. The plot arc may involve complications, conflicts, and setbacks that the characters must overcome in order to achieve their goals.

Overall, the arc of a story is what gives it direction and meaning, and it's what keeps the reader or viewer engaged and invested in the story.

Detroit is a city in the United States that used to be very busy and full of people. However, over time, many things happened that caused the city to become less populated and less busy.

One reason is that the economy of the city changed. Detroit used to be a place where many people worked in factories and made things like cars. But as time passed, these factories closed or moved to other places, and many people lost their jobs. This made it hard for people to stay in Detroit and they had to move to other cities to find work.

Another reason is that the city had a lot of problems with crime and poverty. This made it a less desirable place to live, and many people chose to move to other cities where they felt safer and had more opportunities.

Finally, the city had a lot of old and dilapidated houses and buildings that were not well taken care of. This made it a less attractive place to live, and many people decided to move away.

All of these things combined to cause Detroit to become less populated and less busy over time. While the city is still home to many people, it is not as crowded and bustling as it used to be.

I own quite a few properties used solely for investment . First off , you make money by charging tenants ( renters ) for living in your house . Depending on the terms , the rent can cover utilities or the utilities can be payed by the tenant every month / week / fortnight . To start , all you need is a property that let 's you rent to tenants . If it 's an apartment , most management ( body corporate ) will limit the tenants you 're allowed to have to a certain number ; however , if it 's a house , it 's up to your discretion . To get started , first , of course , get a unit ( property ) under your name . Next , speak to a lawyer about drafting a tenancy agreement - this will legally bind your tenants to the rules you set out for them for renting . After that , list your property with an agent or online to find tenants . If you are renting the unit yourself and want to rent out a room ( or the entire unit ) then it 's called subletting . You 'll need to check with your landlord and look at your tenancy agreement to see if this is allowed . Tips : 1 ) If you own the unit but are still paying mortgage , try to make the amount you rent the property for equivalent or greater than your monthly payments to the bank , that way you 'll basically be buying the house for how much your down - payment was and possibly making profit . 2 ) It 's always easier if you 're in the country or area where you 're managing the properties . I move around quite a bit so I have a agent help me out when needed . 3 ) Do n't skimp on the legal fees for tenancy agreement contracts , a solid contract could save you a lot of time , effort , and money in the future . 4 ) Talk to your tenants before agreeing to let them rent . Knowing that your unit is in good hands in always comforting . If you have any more questions just pm me or reply to this message ! Hope this helps !

Here 's an attempt at a LI5 answer which says more than " most upvotes in littlest time . " Imagine a racetrack for horses that goes on forever in one direction . The start of the track is labeled " Jan 1 , 1970 12:00am " and there are equally spaced notches labeled " Jan 1 , 1970 12:01am " , " Jan 1 , 1970 12:02am " , and so on . When you submit a post , a horse representative is dropped onto the racetrack at the time of submission . If I submit a post now , it will be dropped at the marker reading " Aug 15 , 2011 1:02am . " Now hopefully , my horse will start running ! Every time my post gets an upvote , it gets a little burst of energy and moves forward . Every time my post gets a downvote , it trips up and loses some distance . If it does n't get much attention , it 'll stay around the marker of the time it was submitted . But if it gets lots of upvotes , it will start to race faster and faster , even past the horses that are being dropped onto the track upstream for newer posts ! * * The front page of r / all is a top 25 list of the horses currently winning this race . * * The front page of a given subreddit is the top 25 list of the horses * from that subreddit * currently in the lead . Even the very best submissions will stop getting upvotes after a while , so the horse will start to slow down and eventually come to a stop . Even if it ran out way in front of the other horses , newer horses will have the advantage of being dropped further along the racetrack , so even if my post got a whole ton of upvotes in its lifetime , it wo n't stay in the top 25 for much longer than a couple hours . To make up for this , there 's also the " top all time " option , which lists the horses who traveled the longest distance , so the time they were submitted plays no role anymore .

There are several possible causes of pain behind the knees. It is important for your grandson to seek medical attention to determine the specific cause of his symptoms and to receive appropriate treatment. Some possible causes of pain behind the knees include:

Popliteal artery entrapment syndrome: This condition occurs when the popliteal artery, which supplies blood to the lower leg, becomes compressed or trapped behind the knee. This can cause pain, numbness, and tingling in the lower leg.

Baker's cyst: A Baker's cyst, also known as a popliteal cyst, is a fluid-filled sac that can develop behind the knee. This can cause swelling and pain behind the knee, as well as difficulty bending the knee.

Tendinitis: Inflammation of the tendons in the back of the knee can cause pain and stiffness.

Arthritis: Osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis can both cause pain and swelling in the joints, including the knee.

If your grandson is experiencing sharp pain, tingling, and difficulty moving his legs, he should seek medical attention as soon as possible. It is always better to err on the side of caution when it comes to potential health concerns. An emergency room or urgent care clinic would be a good place for him to go for a thorough evaluation and treatment.

The information for a webpage is stored on a computer called a "server." When you type a web address (like "www.google.com") into your web browser, your computer sends a message to the server asking for the webpage. The server then sends the webpage back to your computer, and your web browser displays it on the screen. So if you make a webpage and no one looks at it, it's still stored on the server, just like how you can still see a book on a shelf if no one is reading it. The server is like a very big bookshelf that stores all the webpages on the internet.

There is actually some science behind this phenomenon! When you move the cords around, they can create a type of energy called kinetic energy. This energy can cause the cords to twist and turn in different directions, which can lead to tangles. 
Sometimes, the tangles can be so bad that it's hard to untangle them. But don't worry, there are a few things you can try to help untangle your headphones. Immanuel Kant was a philosopher who believed that there are certain actions that are always wrong, no matter the circumstances. These actions are called moral duties, and one of them is the duty to tell the truth. According to Kant, lying is always wrong because it goes against this moral duty. Imagine that you are playing a game with your friends and you are trying to hide a toy from them. One of your friends asks you where the toy is, and you know that it is hidden under the couch. If you tell your friend the truth, then you are being honest and following your moral duty to tell the truth. But if you lie and tell your friend that the toy is somewhere else, then you are breaking your moral duty to tell the truth. Kant would say that this is wrong, no matter what the circumstances are. So to answer your question, Kant would say that lying is always wrong, no matter where you are or what you are doing. It is important to always be honest and tell the truth, even if it might be difficult or cause problems. This is what Kant means when he says "Do not lie."

Arietta is , I believe , a Spanish surname ( " Ariette " would be French , or else the plural of the Italian word " arietta " which means " short aria " ) , and would describe a family that originated in the village of Arietta , which is in the Basque region of Spain . The name of the village comes from the Basque " Harrieta " which means " stony place " . There are many different types of surname . Some are derived from the given name of an ancestor , others from the place the family comes from , still others describe an ancestor 's physical characteristic or , more rarely , their personality , and others describe a profession . Some names began as nicknames or even insults . In many cultures -- European cultures certainly -- families were forced to adopt surnames , sometimes against their will , in order to streamline the collection of taxes and track inherited wealth . This sometimes leads to some anomalies : for example , when the English made the Welsh adopt surnames , many Welsh families opted for the common English surname " Jones " ( which means " son of John " ) even though there was no " J " in the Welsh alphabet . People simply had to come up with a name , and different people used different strategies . For example , " Rothschild " comes from German , and is actually pronounced roughly " rote - shilt " : it literally means " red sign " , and comes from the 16th - century practice of identifying houses by using differently coloured signs and symbols . The name " Hoover " is the anglicised version of the German " Huber " , and this word means a person who owns a hide of land -- a hide in Germany was the area of land that could support one family ( something in the region of 20 acres or so ) . So " Hoover " essentially means " comfortably well - off landowner who can support his whole family " . Another German surname is Wagner , denoted somebody who makes carts -- which means that the name of the composer best known for his epic Ring Cycle , especially the piece called [ * The Ride of the Valkyries * ] ( URL_0 ) , translates as " Dick Cartwright " . Meanwhile , the Italian Giuseppe Verdi , famous for such classics as * Rigoletto * , translates as " Joe Green " ; as a surname , " Green " probably indicates somebody who lived in the country .

Generally speaking , the Queen has no real power . But that does n't mean that in a technical sense , she is not still the ruler of the country . Parliament and the ministers , technically speaking , run the country which , technically speaking again , belongs to the queen . Over time , the monarch became less and less involved and more and more authority was divested to Parliament . This process sort of began with the Magna Carta , and slowly dragged on over the centuries . It continued in earnest in the late 1600s , as monarchs stopped vetoing bills from parliament , the position of the prime minister solidified , and such . Queen Victoria , at the start of her reign DID have some power . She successfully blocked a Prime Minister from assuming office . The monarchs had some powerful diplomatic power in the 1800s , brokering alliances and whatnot . The aftermath of WWI was the end(ish ) of the process , as at the time , many monarchies had fallen , and King George backed into a purely ceremonial position . But , and this is important , even after all of that , she is still the queen , and a lot of those things were done voluntarily . There are many things she can still do . She can dissolve the government , remove the prime minister , declare war , break treaties , etc . She can do these thing . Officially . This is because , at the end of the day , unlike in the United States where all of the power of the government officially comes from the Constitution , and the Constitution 's authority comes from " the people , " in the UK , the government 's power comes from he queen , and the queen 's power comes from God . Officially . Unofficially , the queen can not do any of these things . We think ? If the government turned into some fascist dictatorship or something else truly dangerous , it 's possible she might try to exercise her powers , but who knows what would happen ? Remember , heating is n't uniform through out a liquid and the bit you 're sampling the temperature of wo n't be representative of the entire lot . However ! Yes , if you have a volume of water and somehow uniformly make it all hit 100C , it would all become vapour . At 99C technically none of the water will evaporate with no external forces acting on it . But due to the flux of energy between molecules , this 99C is more an average and at any one time a molecule will " transfer energy " to another temporarily ( making a molecule 98C and another 100C ) , giving it enough energy to change phase to a gas form . Put very simply , this is why warm water ( eg 40C ) will evaporate faster than cool water ( 10C ) ; due to this random moving of energy between molecules and the likelihood of one having high enough energy to evaporate . This is of course well before other factors ( relative humidity , layering , pressure etc . ) get taken into account .

Sure! Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness in a system. Imagine you have a box with a bunch of balls in it, and you want to know how mixed up or disordered the balls are. If all the balls are the same color and they're all evenly distributed in the box, then the system has low entropy. If the balls are different colors and they're all mixed up randomly, then the system has high entropy.

So, in simple terms, entropy is a way of measuring how mixed up or disordered something is. It's often used in the context of thermodynamics, which is the study of heat and energy. For example, when you heat up a gas, the gas particles will start moving around faster and become more disordered. This increase in disorder is called an increase in entropy.

The number of electrons that can occupy a given atomic shell is determined by the maximum number of electrons that can have the same set of quantum numbers, which is described by the Pauli exclusion principle. According to this principle, no two electrons in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers.

The maximum number of electrons that can occupy a shell is given by the formula 2n^2, where n is the principal quantum number of the shell. For example, the first shell, with n = 1, can hold a maximum of 2(1^2) = 2 electrons. The second shell, with n = 2, can hold a maximum of 2(2^2) = 8 electrons, and so on.

The arrangement of electrons in the shells of an atom is determined by the Aufbau principle, which states that electrons occupy the lowest energy levels first. This means that electrons will fill the shells of an atom starting with the innermost shell and working outward, filling each shell before moving on to the next.

The number of electrons in each shell can also be determined by using the periodic table, which lists the number of valence electrons for each element. Valence electrons are the electrons in the outermost shell of an atom, and they play a key role in determining the chemical properties of an element.

They work in a variety of medical settings, including hospitals, clinics, and private practices, and they can specialize in any area of medicine.

Bleaching something means using a chemical agent to remove or lighten the color of a substance. When you bleach something, the color does not disappear or dissolve. Instead, the chemical reactions that occur during the bleaching process alter the chemical makeup of the substance, which changes the way it absorbs and reflects light.

Here's a simple explanation of how bleaching works:

Imagine that the substance you want to bleach is like a sponge that absorbs certain colors of light. When the sponge is wet, it looks a certain color because it is absorbing some colors of light and reflecting others. When you apply a bleach solution to the sponge, the bleach changes the chemical makeup of the sponge in such a way that it no longer absorbs the same colors of light. Instead, it absorbs different colors of light or reflects more light, which makes it appear lighter or white.

So, to answer your question, when you bleach something, the color does not disappear or dissolve. Instead, the chemical reactions that occur during the bleaching process alter the chemical makeup of the substance, which changes the way it absorbs and reflects light. This makes the substance appear lighter or white.

Sure! Centrifugal force and centripetal force are both types of forces that can affect the movement of an object.

Centrifugal force is a force that pushes an object away from the center of a circle or curve that it is moving in. Imagine you are riding a swing. As you swing back and forth, you feel like you are being pushed out of the swing. This is because the swing is moving in a circular path, and your body is being pushed outward by the centrifugal force.

Centripetal force, on the other hand, is a force that pulls an object towards the center of a circle or curve that it is moving in. Imagine you are riding a carnival ride that spins around in a circle. As the ride spins, you feel like you are being pulled towards the center. This is because the ride is moving in a circular path, and your body is being pulled inward by the centripetal force.

So, to summarize: Centrifugal force pushes an object away from the center of a circle, while centripetal force pulls an object towards the center of a circle.

A ouija board is a flat board marked with the letters of the alphabet, the numbers 0 through 9, the words "yes," "no," and "goodbye," and various other symbols and words. People often use a ouija board as a way to try to communicate with the spirits of deceased people.

To use a ouija board, people usually sit around the board with their fingers lightly resting on a small, heart-shaped piece of wood or plastic called a planchette. They then ask the board questions and wait for the planchette to move, spelling out answers by sliding over the letters and numbers on the board.

There is no scientific evidence that the ouija board can be used to communicate with spirits, and many people believe that the movements of the planchette are simply the result of the users unconsciously moving it themselves. This is known as the "ideomotor effect," which is the phenomenon of people unconsciously moving their muscles in response to certain suggestions or ideas. So, while using a ouija board with trustworthy people may be a fun or interesting activity, it is not a reliable way to communicate with the spirits of deceased individuals.

Context effects of memory refer to the ways in which the context in which information is encountered or learned can influence the way that information is later remembered. There are several different types of context effects that can influence memory, including:

Encoding context effects: These refer to the influence of the physical, emotional, or social context in which information is first encountered on the way that information is later remembered. For example, if you learn information while you are in a calm and relaxed state, you may be more likely to remember it later than if you were in a stressed or anxious state.

Retrieval context effects: These refer to the influence of the context in which information is retrieved on the way that information is remembered. For example, if you try to remember something you learned in a particular place, you may be more likely to remember it if you are in that same place when you try to retrieve the information.

The von Restorff effect: This is a specific type of context effect that occurs when an item that stands out in some way (e.g., because it is in a different color or font) is more likely to be remembered than items that are more similar to the other items in the list.

The serial position effect: This is another specific type of context effect that occurs when items that are presented at the beginning or end of a list are more likely to be remembered than items that are presented in the middle of the list.

Overall, context effects of memory are important because they demonstrate the ways in which the context in which information is encountered or retrieved can influence the way that information is stored and retrieved in memory.

It is worth saying , that directly propelling a boat with wind such that the wind speed is propelling a boat in the same direction is not normally what happens . Not often . In the days of square rigged tall ships , this was more often the case . In that case , the boat can not exceed the wind speed ( wind propelling boat from behind ) without a current propelling the boat fast than the wind , at which point the wind would be slowing the boat . Most often , you are sailing on some angle where the boat is into the wind from say 9 oclock to 3 oclock , but never directly into 12 oclock . This does create lift over the sail . However the physics are a little more complicated . There is the water that the boat keel , fin , skeg , foil etc is displacing , the wind , the weight of the boat are all vectors that result in a vector sum in the resultant direction . This is further complicated by the drag of air and water , wave motion , etc etc . TLDR , the forward motion of a boat is a vector sum and goes faster than wind when sailing some angle between against and perpendicular to the direction of the wind . URL_0

Programming languages are a way for humans to give instructions to computers. Different programming languages are designed to be used for different types of tasks, and they all have their own set of rules and syntax (the way instructions are written).

C++ is a programming language that is often used for creating large, complex programs, such as operating systems and video games. It is a compiled language, which means that it is converted into a form that the computer can understand and execute before it is run. C++ is known for being fast and efficient, but it can be difficult to learn and use.

Objective-C is another programming language that is often used for creating software for Apple's Mac and iOS operating systems. It is a dynamic language, which means that it is interpreted at runtime (when the program is being run) rather than being compiled ahead of time. Objective-C is known for being easy to read and write, but it can be slower than compiled languages like C++.

There are many different programming languages because each one has its own strengths and weaknesses, and is best suited for different types of tasks. For example, Python is a popular language for data analysis and machine learning, while Java is often used for building Android apps. There is no one "best" programming language - it all depends on what you want to do with it.

Sure! A scientific law is a statement that describes a pattern that we observe in nature. It's like a rule that explains how the natural world works. A scientific theory, on the other hand, is a more detailed explanation for why a scientific law is true. For example, let's say we have a scientific law that says "objects fall towards the ground when we drop them." This is a pattern that we observe every time we drop something, so it's a scientific law. But why do objects fall towards the ground? Well, that's where a scientific theory comes in. There could be a few reasons why your WiFi signal is degrading over time. Here are a few possible explanations:

Interference: There are many things that can interfere with your WiFi signal, such as other electronic devices, walls, and even fish tanks.

Distance: The further you are from your router, the weaker the signal will be.

Obstructions: If there are physical objects between your device and the router, such as walls or furniture, they can block or weaken the signal.

Router location: Where you place your router can make a big difference in the strength of your WiFi signal. It's best to keep the router in a central location and as high up as possible.

Router age: As routers age, they can become less effective at transmitting a strong signal. It's possible that your router is simply old and needs to be replaced.

Router settings: There could be something wrong with your router's settings that is causing the signal to degrade. Resetting or rebooting your router might help, but it's also a good idea to check the settings to make sure everything is configured properly.

I hope this helps to explain the possible causes of a degraded WiFi signal. If you continue to have trouble, you may want to consider consulting a professional or purchasing a newer, more powerful router.

A hash function is a mathematical process that takes an input (like a password) and turns it into an output of a fixed length (called a hash value or hash). The important thing about a hash function is that it is a one-way process. This means that you can't take the output (the hash) and "reverse" it to get the original input (the password).

Imagine a box that you can put things into, but once they're in, you can't get them out. That's like a hash function. You can put the password into the box (by running it through the hash function), but you can't get the password back out of the box. All you have is the hash value, which is just a bunch of numbers and letters.

The only way to figure out what the original password was is to try lots of different passwords, run them through the hash function, and see if the output (the hash) matches the one you have. This process is called "brute force guessing" because you're just trying lots of different things until you find the right one.

It's like trying to open a locked box without the key. You can try different combinations of numbers on the lock, but it's going to take you a long time to find the right combination, especially if the lock has a lot of numbers to choose from. That's why it's important to use long, strong passwords that are hard to guess, because it makes it much harder for someone to use brute force guessing to figure out your password.

The first digit represents a larger region of the country, while the last four digits represent a smaller area within that region. For example, a zip code that starts with "9" is likely to be on the west coast of the United States, while a zip code that starts with "1" is likely to be on the east coast. Sometimes you may see the same zip code on different addresses. This is because the zip code is assigned based on the location of the post office that will deliver the mail, rather than the location of the person or business receiving the mail. The term "20/20" is used to describe normal vision, which means that a person is able to see clearly at a distance of 20 feet what should normally be seen at that distance. It is a standard used to measure visual acuity, which is the clarity or sharpness of your vision.

For example, if your vision is 20/40, it means that you need to be as close as 20 feet to see what a person with normal vision can see clearly at a distance of 40 feet. This means that your vision is not as sharp as someone with 20/20 vision.

The same measurement system is used for both nearsightedness and farsightedness. Nearsightedness means that you can see things clearly up close, but things that are farther away appear blurry. Farsightedness means that you can see things clearly from far away, but things that are up close appear blurry.

The treadmill measures the force and speed at which you push back the conveyor belt , which allows it to calculate the mechanical work you 're doing , in watts . Usually , the treadmill actually displays this number . Power is energy per second , so it can be converted into energy in joules per hour by multiplying it with 3600 , and then dividing it by 4.2 to go from joules to calories . So 100 w of mechanical power translates to 100\*3600/4.2 = 86 ( kilo ) calories per hour . That 's the easy part . The difficult part is to figure out the amount of calories burned for the mechanical energy you produce . This is most likely a proprietary formula that varies with each manufacturer . The machines in my gym just seem to multiply it with 7 though , so the 100 w in my example translate to 600 calories per hour . Edit : Just in case you were wondering : What we call calories are actually kilo calories , which is why they are abbreviated " kcal " .

As you almost said in your question , the act of evaporation involves the water molecules getting enough energy so that the water can take its gasous form . The key part here is " water molecules " . When water evaporates , nothing else gets " picked up " with it . Each molecule is always by definition pure H2O. Once up in the atmosphere this water vapour condenses again and forms clouds . Now that it has a tangible form ( i.e. it 's become liquid again ) , other things that are in the atmosphere can dissolve in it once more . This will usually be other gases such as carbon dioxide . Finally , when the condensed water vapour joins into drops large enough to fall due to gravity , we get rain . As the rain descends it might pass through dust and other solid pollutants , some of which will also dissolve into the rain . So by the time a raindrop lands back on earth it 's unlikely to be pure H2O , even though that 's how it started out when it first evaporated . You 'll notice that salt is not mentioned in this description . Salt molecules remain most definitely solid at the sort of temperatures you encounter at the surface of the earth ( common salt , NaCl melts at 800 ° C/1500 ° F ) and therefore when the water evaporates they get left behind . Incidentally this fact is one of the primary means by which salt is " harvested " -- seawater is poured into large containers and the water allowed to evaporate . The compounds left behind consist of various salts but primarily NaCl .

In mathematics, the factorial of a number is a way of representing the product of a series of numbers. The factorial of a positive integer n is denoted by n!, and it is defined as the product of all the positive integers from 1 up to n. So, for example, 5! = 1 * 2 * 3 * 4 * 5 = 120.

Now, the factorial of 0, denoted by 0!, is defined as being equal to 1. This may seem strange at first, since there are no positive integers less than zero, but it actually makes sense when you think about it in the context of how factorials are used.

One common use of factorials is in counting problems, where you want to find the total number of ways that a certain number of items can be arranged or grouped. For example, if you have three letters (A, B, and C), there are 3! = 6 different ways that you can arrange them: ABC, ACB, BAC, BCA, CAB, and CBA.

Now, imagine that you have no items at all. In this case, there is only one way to arrange them: there are no items, so there is only one arrangement. This is why 0! is defined as being equal to 1.

Tycho Brahe was a Danish astronomer who made a number of important contributions to the field of astronomy during the late 16th and early 17th centuries. He is perhaps most famous for his careful and accurate observations of the positions and movements of the celestial bodies, which he used to challenge the long-held belief that the Earth was the center of the universe.

Brahe was born in 1546 in Denmark, and from a young age he showed a keen interest in the stars and the sky. He received a classical education in a number of subjects, including mathematics, physics, and astronomy, and he began to make his own observations of the celestial bodies using the tools and techniques available at the time.

Brahe is known for his careful and precise observations of the positions and movements of the planets and stars, which he made using a number of sophisticated instruments that he designed and built himself. He was particularly interested in the planets, and he made a number of important discoveries about their behavior, including the fact that they orbited the sun in elliptical rather than circular orbits.

In addition to his observational work, Brahe also made a number of important contributions to the development of the scientific method and the advancement of knowledge about the universe. He was a strong advocate for the use of empirical evidence in scientific investigation, and he believed that theories should be tested and refined through careful observation and experimentation.

Despite his many contributions to the field of astronomy, Brahe is perhaps best remembered for his role in paving the way for the work of his contemporary, Johannes Kepler, who used Brahe's observations to develop his laws of planetary motion.

Hibernation is a way that some animals, like bears and groundhogs, sleep through the winter when there is not much food available. When an animal hibernates, its body slows down and it doesn't need to eat or drink as much. Its heart rate and breathing also slow down, so the animal doesn't use up as much energy. During hibernation, the animal's body temperature also drops. This helps it to conserve energy because it doesn't have to use as much energy to keep warm. Some animals, like bears, do wake up during their hibernation period, but they usually don't move around much. They might stretch or get a drink of water, but then they go back to sleep. Other animals, like groundhogs, might not wake up at all during their hibernation period. So, to answer your question, animals that hibernate are able to sleep for a long time without needing to eat or poop because their bodies are able to conserve energy and they don't need as much food or water. They might wake up during their hibernation period, but they usually don't do much except go back to sleep.

It's not uncommon for hospitals to have poor cell phone reception, and there are a few reasons why this might be the case. One reason is that hospitals are often large buildings with thick walls made of materials that can block or weaken cell phone signals. Additionally, hospitals often have a lot of electronic equipment that can interfere with cell phone signals. Finally, hospitals often have strict rules about the use of electronic devices, which can make it difficult to install cell phone boosters or other types of signal-enhancing equipment. Overall, the main reason why hospitals often have poor cell phone reception is because of the materials used in their construction and the presence of electronic equipment, which can make it difficult for cell phone signals to penetrate the building.

"Talk is cheap" is a saying that means that it's easy to say something, but it's harder to actually do it. For example, if someone says they will help you move, but they never actually show up to help, they are just talking and not actually doing anything. The saying suggests that it's better to show someone that you mean what you say by taking action, rather than just talking about it.

Another way to think about it is that words are cheap, but actions are more valuable. It's easy to say that you will do something, but it's more meaningful to actually do it. So, when someone says "talk is cheap," they are saying that it's important to pay more attention to what people do, rather than just listening to what they say.

Water damage can cause a number of problems with electronic devices such as phones and laptops. When water gets inside the device, it can cause short circuits, which are electrical connections that happen unexpectedly. These short circuits can cause the device to stop working or function improperly. 
Water can also cause corrosion, which is when the metal parts of the device start to break down and deteriorate. This can make the device stop working properly, or even completely stop working. 
Additionally, water can cause the device to rust, which is when the metal parts start to turn red and become covered in a reddish-brown substance. Rust can also cause the device to stop working properly or stop working altogether. 
Finally, water can cause the device to malfunction or stop working if it gets into the device's hardware, such as the motherboard or other important components. These components are responsible for controlling the device's functions, and if they become damaged by water, the device may stop working.

It is generally recommended to have a doctor evaluate a child after any fall, especially if the child was crying hard or is not acting like themselves. Babies at this age are at increased risk for head injuries because their head is larger and heavier relative to their body and their neck muscles are not fully developed, which can make it difficult for them to control their head movement during a fall. Even if there are no visible marks or lumps on the head, it is possible for a child to sustain a concussion or other head injury. If the child is not acting like themselves or is showing any concerning symptoms, such as vomiting, headache, dizziness, or lethargy, it is especially important to have them evaluated by a doctor.

It is also a good idea to make sure the child is up to date on their vaccinations, as some of the vaccines protect against serious illnesses that could be contracted from falls.

In the meantime, it is important to observe the child closely and seek medical attention if you notice any concerning symptoms. It is always better to be safe than sorry when it comes to head injuries in young children.

The scanners use radiation which penetrates your clothes but not your skin . It bounces off much the same way light bounces off regular objects , so all you need is a machine that can see these wavelengths , plus an operator who can look at the scans and see the outline of a gun or knife . One fairly discerning thing is that you do n't have to conceal things inside your body for the scanner to miss them , just hang it off the side . And keep in mind that these operators see thousands of scans per shift , so it 's not surprising that they miss stuff all the time . In other words this is primarily ' security theater ' , something meant to look like it works but is actually easy to fool . Plus it 's dosing you with radiation , not much to be sure , but radiation is n't good for you .

Rain has a distinctive smell because it washes away pollutants and other particles from the air as it falls to the ground. When it rains, the water droplets pick up these particles and carry them to the ground, where they can be washed away. As the rain falls, it releases these particles into the air, and we can smell them.

The smell of rain is also caused by a chemical called ozone, which is created when lightning splits oxygen molecules in the air during a thunderstorm. Ozone has a strong, clean smell that is often described as "fresh" or "electric."

So when you smell rain, you are actually smelling a combination of the particles that have been washed out of the air by the rain, as well as the ozone created by the lightning.

To measure the valuation or devaluation of a currency in relation to itself, you can use the exchange rate between the currency and itself. The exchange rate is the price of one currency in terms of another currency.For example, if the exchange rate between the US dollar and the US dollar is 1:1, this means that one US dollar is equal to one US dollar. In this case, the US dollar is neither being valued nor devalued.If the exchange rate between the US dollar and the US dollar is greater than 1:1, this means that the US dollar is being valued relative to itself. For example, if the exchange rate is 1.10:1, this means that one US dollar is worth 1.10 US dollars.On the other hand, if the exchange rate between the US dollar and the US dollar is less than 1:1, this means that the US dollar is being devalued relative to itself. For example, if the exchange rate is 0.90:1, this means that one US dollar is worth 0.90 US dollars.It's important to note that the exchange rate between a currency and itself will always be equal to 1. The exchange rate is used to compare the value of one currency to another, not to itself.

"Meta" is a prefix that means "about" or "beyond." So, something that is "meta" is about itself or beyond itself.

A "self-referential" thing is something that refers to itself. This means that it talks about itself or mentions itself in some way.

For example, if you say "I am saying this sentence right now," you are being self-referential because you are mentioning yourself (I) in the sentence.

Here's another example: if you make a joke about how bad your jokes are, that's a self-referential joke because it's talking about itself (the joke).

Creating a keygen, short for "key generator," is a process that involves reverse engineering software and then creating a program that can generate valid product keys for that software. Keygens are often used by hackers to distribute pirated copies of software. 
To create a keygen, a hacker will first obtain a copy of the software that they want to crack. They will then use various tools and techniques to analyze the software and figure out how it works and how it checks for valid product keys. This can involve disassembling the software's code and examining it, or using specialized software to analyze the software's behavior. 
Once the hacker has a good understanding of how the software works and how it checks for valid product keys, they can create a program that generates keys that will pass this check. This program is called a keygen. 
Keygens can be created quickly if the hacker is skilled and has access to the right tools and resources. However, creating a keygen is a time-consuming and complex process, and it is not something that can be done in a few hours. It usually takes a significant amount of time and effort to create a keygen for a new piece of software.

If you are selling goods or services to customers in the United States, you may be required to pay taxes on your income. The specific tax obligations you have will depend on your business structure, the state where your customers are located, and the type of goods or services you are selling.In general, if you are a nonresident of the United States, you may be required to pay taxes on your income from selling goods or services to customers in the United States if you have a "permanent establishment" in the country. A permanent establishment is a fixed place of business through which you carry on your business activities. It could be a physical location, such as a store or office, or it could be an online platform or website.If you do have a permanent establishment in the United States and you are selling goods or services to customers in the country, you may be required to file a tax return and pay taxes on your income. You may also be required to collect and pay sales tax on your sales to customers in certain states. It is important to note that the rules for collecting and paying sales tax can vary significantly from state to state.To determine your specific tax obligations as a nonresident selling goods or services to customers in the United States, it is recommended that you consult with a tax professional or research the applicable tax laws and regulations.

There are a few measures that actors may go through when filming a sex scene to ensure that everyone is comfortable and safe. For example, actors may use body doubles (stand-ins who look similar to the actors) for certain parts of the scene, or they may use special clothing or props to cover up certain parts of their bodies. Actors may also have a closed set, where only necessary crew members are present, to create a more private and intimate atmosphere. The wash sale rule is a tax rule that is designed to prevent taxpayers from claiming losses on the sale of securities when they do not actually experience an economic loss. The rule applies to the sale of securities that are held in a taxable account, such as a brokerage account or an individual retirement account (IRA).Under the wash sale rule, if a taxpayer sells securities at a loss and then buys substantially identical securities within 30 days before or after the sale, the taxpayer is not allowed to claim the loss on their tax return. Instead, the loss is deferred until the taxpayer sells the replacement securities. The wash sale rule applies to both long-term and short-term capital losses.The purpose of the wash sale rule is to prevent taxpayers from artificially generating tax losses by selling securities at a loss and then immediately buying them back. By disallowing these losses, the wash sale rule helps to ensure that taxpayers pay taxes on the actual economic gains or losses they experience when they sell securities.

Every time this is asked , you 'll get a bunch of bad information . No child left behind was a federal law that said that each state had to test their students in math , reading , and eventually science . You have to give a test to get federally funding . They wo n't take away money for doing poorly . You just have to take it . What it did do was you had to make yearly progress towards a goal of having 100 % of your students " proficient " by 2013 . That was obviously a completely ridiculous goal as there 's no way you 're ever going to get 100 % of the kids in America proficient in every academic subject . So you had to make adequate progress . Lets say the first year you take it , 30 % of your kids pass . Next year , your goal would be to have 40 % of your kids pass . Now , if you fail to meet your goal , then you start getting some consequences . If a school misses their goal for four years , it could be taken over and the staff replaced . Now there are debates about the positives or negatives that I do n't really feel like getting into but I felt the need to correct some misinformation that is popping up yet again . I 'll be glad to answer any other questions !

It 's honestly the same basic story that 's been told for centuries . A savior / chosen one arrives on the scene to save the world . Like other very successful spins on this basic story ( eg , Lord of the Rings , Star Wars , The Hunger Games ) , its success stems from Rowling 's ability to create a unique world and populate it with characters we can root for and against . I think what separates Rowling is that even though her story is in a very fantastical universe , it 's still relatable to all ages . We all attended primary school / middle school / high school , so it 's easy to recognize the awkward first kiss , the school bully and the teacher everyone hated . And who would n't want to attend Wizarding school at Hogwarts , play Quidditch , drink Butterbeer , and learn how to cast spells to thwart our enemies ? Middle school in particular is one of the more awkward , miserable times for most people and Rowling offered an alternate reality where school is actually fun , your best friends really are forever and you are empowered to actually change things .

Carfax is a company that provides information on the history of used cars. They get their data from a variety of sources, including: Auto dealerships: Many dealerships will provide Carfax with information on the cars they have for sale, including the make, model, year, and any repairs or maintenance that have been done. Mechanics and repair shops: These businesses may provide Carfax with information on the work they have done on a particular car, such as oil changes, brakes, or other repairs. State Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV): The DMV keeps records of all vehicles registered in the state, including any accidents or traffic violations. Carfax can access this information and include it in their reports. Insurance companies: Insurance companies may also provide Carfax with information on cars that have been in accidents or have had damage. Carfax pays for this information from these sources. They use this information to create a detailed history report on each car, which can help potential buyers make informed decisions when purchasing a used car.

If the previous owner of your home wants to buy it back and the property's value is less than the amount of your loan, you have a few options to consider. Here are some potential options:Sell the home to the previous owner for the current market value: If the previous owner is willing to pay the current market value of the home, even if it is less than the amount of your loan, you may be able to sell the home to them. This could allow you to recoup some of your investment and move on from the property.Consider a short sale: If the previous owner is not willing or able to pay the full amount of the loan, you may be able to negotiate a short sale with your lender. This involves selling the home for less than the amount of the loan and obtaining permission from the lender to do so. In a short sale, the lender agrees to forgive the remaining balance on the loan.Try to refinance the loan: If you are unable to sell the home or negotiate a short sale, you may be able to refinance the loan to lower the monthly payment. This could involve extending the term of the loan or obtaining a lower interest rate.It's important to carefully consider your options and weigh the pros and cons of each before making a decision. You may want to consult with a financial advisor or real estate attorney for guidance on the best course of action for your specific situation.

In situations where the username is used as a displayed name , case sensitive usernames would enable someone to attempt to impersonate someone else just by registering a new account with a different permutation of capital / lowercase letters . The same applies for web domains . For emails , case sensitive addresses would result in a lot of undelivered emails from someone forgetting which letters were capitalized or not . Etc . Also worth noting that some companies , like Wells Fargo , do not use case sensitive passwords as an added convenience to the user . For passwords , passwords are supposed to be arbitrary , are not stored in plain text or ever displayed , and allowing case sensitive passwords enables a massively wider range of possible passwords to discourage guessing .

In England, the monarchy is a form of government in which a king or queen serves as the head of state. The monarchy has a long history in England, dating back to the Middle Ages. The current monarch in England is Queen Elizabeth II.

The Queen does not have any real political power, but she performs important ceremonial and symbolic duties. For example, she officially opens Parliament each year and gives a speech outlining the government's plans for the coming year. She also represents England on state visits to other countries, and meets with foreign dignitaries who visit England.

The Queen and other members of the royal family also carry out charitable work and support a variety of causes. They are also seen as important symbols of national unity and pride.

Although the Queen has no real political power, she is an important figure in English culture and history. Many people in England are interested in the royal family and enjoy following their activities and traditions.

A lighter doesn't explode when it's lit because the flame from the lighter is not hot enough to cause the gas inside the lighter to explode. If the flame were any hotter, it could potentially cause the gas to explode, but the lighter is designed so that this doesn't happen.

As an Indian citizen working in the United States, you may be subject to tax in both countries on your worldwide income. The United States has a tax treaty with India that may affect how you are taxed in each country.In general, if you are a resident of India for tax purposes, you will be subject to Indian tax on your worldwide income. If you are a resident of the United States for tax purposes, you will be subject to US tax on your worldwide income. However, the tax treaty between the United States and India may provide some relief from double taxation.Under the tax treaty, if you are a resident of India and you are present in the United States for 183 days or more in a calendar year, you will be considered a resident of the United States for tax purposes. In this case, you will be subject to US tax on your worldwide income, but you may be able to claim a credit for any Indian tax paid on the same income.If you are a resident of India and you are present in the United States for less than 183 days in a calendar year, you will generally be considered a nonresident alien for US tax purposes. In this case, you will only be subject to US tax on certain types of US-source income, such as income from wages, salaries, and other personal services performed in the United States. You may also be subject to US tax on certain types of passive income, such as dividends, interest, and rents.It is important to note that the rules for determining tax residency can be complex and depend on your specific circumstances. You should consult with a tax professional or refer to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and the Indian tax authorities for more information on your tax obligations as an Indian citizen working in the United States.

Cancer is a group of diseases that occur when cells in the body grow and divide in an uncontrolled way. These cells can form tumors or abnormal growths and can spread to other parts of the body. Cancer is a very common disease, and it has been around for a long time. In the past, people may not have lived long enough to develop cancer because of diseases or injuries that were more likely to kill them at a younger age. Today, people are living longer, so they have a higher chance of developing cancer. There are many different things that can cause cells to grow uncontrollably and become cancerous. Some of these things include: Exposure to certain substances, such as tobacco smoke, radiation, and certain chemicals
Infection with certain viruses or bacteria
Certain inherited gene mutations
Changes in the way that genes work that occur naturally as we age It's important to note that not everyone who is exposed to these risk factors will develop cancer. There are also many things that people can do to reduce their risk of developing cancer, such as not smoking, eating a healthy diet, and getting regular exercise.

Chest tightness and pain in the left arm can be caused by various conditions, and it is important to seek medical attention if you are experiencing these symptoms. While it is possible that the cause of your symptoms may be benign, it is also possible that they could be caused by a more serious condition such as a heart attack.

Some possible causes of chest tightness and pain in the left arm include:

Heart attack: A heart attack occurs when the blood flow to the heart is blocked, causing damage to the heart muscle. Chest tightness and pain in the left arm are common symptoms of a heart attack.

Angina: Angina is a type of chest pain that occurs when the heart is not getting enough oxygen. It is often described as a feeling of tightness, pressure, or squeezing in the chest. Pain in the left arm can also be a symptom of angina.

Panic attack: Panic attacks are episodes of intense fear that can cause physical symptoms such as chest tightness, pain, and difficulty breathing. Pain in the left arm can also be a symptom of a panic attack.

Musculoskeletal problems: Chest tightness and pain in the left arm can also be caused by musculoskeletal problems such as muscle strain or rib fractures.

It is important to speak with a healthcare professional about your symptoms to determine the cause and get the appropriate treatment. They may recommend additional tests or procedures to diagnose the cause of your symptoms. In the meantime, it is important to avoid strenuous activity and to seek medical attention immediately if you experience any worsening of your symptoms or if you have any new or unusual symptoms.

The poster is asking whether using mouthwash would allow bacteria that have an innate genetic resistance to the ingredients of the mouthwash , to flourish unchallenged in the ecosystem of one 's mouth , leading to an eventual situation of a mouth full of bacteria that ca n't be killed with mouthwash . If the mouthwash used a tricky , specific way to select the bacteria , the way antibiotics like penicillin do , by ( for example ) attaching to specific protein configurations that happen to be on the cell wall of the bacterium so that white blood cells can easily find and devour it — it would possibly allow resistant bacteria to thrive , producing a " bad breath superbug " . The mechanisms used by mouthwash to kill bacteria , however , are more along the lines of rolling a lava flow over them . The bacteria that survive , do so not because they are resistant genetically — they survive because they happened to be hidden in an isolated pocket where the mouthwash could n't get them . Your gum and tongue cells get scorched , too ; they just happen to have a horde of cells waiting behind them . Whichever ones do survive do flourish relatively unchallenged , but there is no genetic difference that they can pass on to subsequent generations ; those will still be just as susceptible to mouthwash . The bad news is that alcohol use has a tendency to kill off your immune system cells , which * do n't * have a horde of cells waiting to replace them , which gives you a slightly increased chance of being infected by any arbitrary pathogen that is in your mouth — which includes viruses that can cause cancer .

First , the body pays special attention to alcohol . It 's a small molecule and gets very quickly through the walls of the gut into the bloodstream and then to the brain . Second , if you weigh 60 kilograms , you generate about 60 milliliters of urine each hour . And for 80 kilograms , about 80 milliliters per hour , and so on . Third , we humans seem to prefer to drink our alcohol in 10 gram lumps . Ten grams of alcohol is about 12.5 milliliters ( but you can call it 10 mL and still be fairly accurate ) . So each glass of beer , wine , or spirits has about 10 grams of alcohol . Fourth , alcohol interferes with the mechanism that regulates the water levels in our body . So now , a little anatomy and physiology . In your brain is a small gland called the pituitary gland . It is divided into two sections : the front ; and the back . The back section is called the posterior pituitary . One of the hormones made by the posterior pituitary gland is called vasopressin , or anti - diuretic hormone ( ADH ) . Diuresis is a fancy word meaning urination . Now suppose that you are really dehydrated . So the volume of water in your body is low . But you still have just as many salts floating in this reduced volume of water . So these salts are now more concentrated in the reduced volume of water that you have when you are dehydrated . Your body has detectors that can sense both the saltiness of your water , and the volume of the water . If these detectors reckon that you are dehydrated , they send a signal to the posterior pituitary gland , which starts pumping out ADH . The job of ADH is to stop you urinating , so you hang on to your precious water . You reduce your normal rate of making urine . Alcohol does the opposite . It reduces how much ADH you make , so it increases how much urine you produce . Each shot of alcohol that you drink forces your kidneys to generate an extra 120 milliliters of urine on top of the normal 60–80 milliliters per hour .

By buying US dollars on the black market, people can get around these controls and obtain more foreign currency than they would be able to legally.To avoid taxes: In some cases, people might buy US dollars on the black market in order to avoid paying taxes on the transaction.To take advantage of fluctuating exchange rates: If the exchange rate for a particular currency is more favorable on the black market than it is through official channels, people might choose to buy US dollars on the black market in order to get a better deal.To access foreign goods and services: In countries where certain goods or services are not readily available or are heavily taxed, people might buy US dollars on the black market in order to purchase these items from abroad.It is important to note that buying or selling currency on the black market can be illegal, and it carries risks such as the potential for scams or fraud, as well as the risk of getting caught and facing legal consequences.

When you walk, your body uses energy to move your muscles and legs. This energy comes from the food you eat, which is converted into a form of energy called ATP (adenosine triphosphate). As you walk, your body uses ATP to power your muscles, and the ATP is then converted into a different form of energy called heat.

Heat is a type of energy that is produced when molecules in an object or substance move around more quickly. When you walk, the muscles in your legs move and produce heat. This heat is a form of energy that is produced as a result of the kinetic energy being used.

So, when you walk, the kinetic energy that your body uses to move is converted into heat energy. This heat energy is then released from your body and can be absorbed by the environment around you. The kinetic energy that you used to walk is not destroyed, but rather it is converted into a different form of energy called heat.

The simplicity mostly comes in the form of HUGE tolerances ( clearances ... sorry ) between parts inside the receiver . If you open an AK up , there 's a ton of empty space inside the receiver , and very few moving parts in the trigger group . That allows you to put all kinds of dirt , gunk , snow , sand , pebbles , dust , etc . inside the thing without gumming up the works . They 're also incredibly easy to maintain because of these loose tolerances and the relatively few parts . If you can open the receiver , dump out any crud that 's accumulated in there , and slather some motor oil over the moving parts , you 're good to go . As to how they became so ubiquitous , that 's partly because of the easy manufacturing process ( the receiver is stamped from a single sheet of metal and bent into form ) , and the fact that the USSR absolutely * loved * to stick its nose into other countries ' business ; even moreso than the USA did . They had a habit of mass producing AKs and arming little pissant rebel groups all across the globe , and the gun worked quite well for that purpose because it 's so easy to maintain , and so resistant to damage and jamming . A barely - trained nobody could be turned from peasant to warrior with the addition of an AK . So basically , it comes down to the fact that the AK was easy to make , easy to maintain , and tough as hell because that 's what it was designed to be . The USSR war ethic at the time was all about mass production of overwhelming force , and the AK was designed to fit in that niche .

The philtrum has a few different functions. For one, it helps to guide liquids and food towards the mouth, making it easier to swallow. It also plays a role in our facial expressions and helps to give our face its unique shape. During fetal development, the philtrum forms when the two sides of the face fuse together. This happens around the ninth week of pregnancy. So, in short, the philtrum is a part of the face that helps with eating and facial expressions, and it forms during fetal development. The total price of an option (strike price + option cost) decreases as the strike price decreases because the option has more intrinsic value when the strike price is lower. Intrinsic value is the difference between the strike price of the option and the current market price of the underlying asset. For example, if the market price of a stock is $100 and the strike price of a call option on that stock is $90, then the intrinsic value of the option is $10 ($100 market price - $90 strike price). This means that the option is already worth at least $10, regardless of whether it is exercised or not.On the other hand, if the strike price of the option is higher, then the intrinsic value of the option is lower. For example, if the strike price of the option is $110, then the intrinsic value is only $0 ($100 market price - $110 strike price). In this case, the option would only have value if the market price of the underlying asset increased above the strike price before the option expired.Because the intrinsic value of an option is an important component of its total price, options with lower strike prices tend to be more expensive than options with higher strike prices. This is why the total price of an option (strike price + option cost) decreases as the strike price decreases.
